prompt: Here's my project brief: we're building a SaaS platform for independent restaurants to manage their online presence — menus, reviews, local SEO, social media scheduling. The codebase already exists with a Next.js frontend in src/app/, Tailwind config at tailwind.config.ts, and a Supabase backend. I need an agent company that covers product strategy, frontend design, content for the marketing site, and developer experience. Maybe 6 agents? I want them properly tested and ready to go.
assertions: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory)
Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger with inclusion and exclusion criteria
Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox
At least one agent references actual codebase paths like src/app/, tailwind.config.ts, or supabase/ in its body
The skill evaluates whether 6 agents is the right number rather than blindly accepting the user's suggestion
The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before final delivery
Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content
Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
